My biggest concern is cost predictability. While pay-per-use sounds flexible, I'm worried that if I use AI regularly, the charges could add up and end up costing more than a monthly subscription. I'd want clear, transparent pricing with no hidden fees so I always know what I'm spending.
Pricing transparency and whether the results will be worth paying for on a regular basis.

The plans should contain/include all kinds of AI Chatbots and also various models of those Chatbots. Also there should be appropriate limitations on usage e.g. tokens, etc. , i.e. it should justify the amount paid by user.

It is very difficult for startup founder or freelancers to anticipate how much ai usage they will have considering the frequency of the  projects they get . Some time it may overshoot the 20 dollar barrier . Also it helps the ai platforms like claude , openai to slap a flat subscription to the users in terms of definet paying customers they have , making business projections easier and reliable .My concern is that whether will they provide you their advance llm access .My concern is that not many people especially in non-tech industry will use your model since their ai usage is very low in quality and quantity . They will prefer the free ones .

1) Tax maybe? Tax on a single query would be close to negligible. For example - 0.1 rupees per query. 18% of that ( arbitrary percentage number) would be really small. Payment processing fees, book keeping and tax compliance on millions of very small transactions could be a pain in general.

2)A major major problem could be the person simply doesnt pay after use. A prepaid wallet could work for this(paying a fixed amount which isnt exactly deducted but kept incase the person doesnt pay).

3)From a personal view I dont pay for AI use. I manage tokens and use LLM's. I am pretty sure more than 90% people using AI do this too. So apart from the payment feature of money per query , creating a simple market place could be integrated too.

My absolute biggest concern is the financial unpredictability and the risk of ultimately paying more than a standard flat-rate subscription. While I love the efficiency and savings of a pay-per-use model during light periods where I might only pay a small fraction instead of eating a full $20 monthly fee I hesitate because there is no built-in safety net for heavy usage. Without a fixed cap, I worry that a busy week could easily rack up a bill that ends up being significantly more expensive than a traditional subscription, making me feel penalized for relying on the AI when I need it most.

I am quite hesitant about potential latency and the handling of large context windows. Since pay per use models usually charge based on input/output tokens, long chat histories or uploading massive PDFs could become incredibly expensive very quickly. Additionally, if this platform routes requests through a third-party API wrapper, I worry about minor speed delays (latency) compared to accessing native platforms like ChatGPT plus or Claude pro directly, as well as how securely my data is being handled
